content:
===BEGIN===
Antibiotics are special medicines that help fight germs called bacteria when they make us sick. But sometimes, these bacteria can change and become strong against the antibiotics. This is called antibiotic resistance.

Imagine you have a superhero shield that protects you from bad guys. If the bad guys learn how to break your shield, they become harder to stop. That’s what happens with bacteria and antibiotics. When people use antibiotics too much or don’t finish their medicine, some bacteria survive and get stronger. These strong bacteria can spread to other people and make it harder to get better.

That’s why doctors say we should only use antibiotics when we really need them and always finish the whole medicine. This helps keep the antibiotics working so they can keep fighting the bad germs and keep us healthy!
